headline: Investors Are Having Doubts About Interest-Rate Cuts
summary: Fears of a prolonged Middle East conflict are leading investors to drastically scale back their bets on interest-rate cuts this year.  Interest-rate futures showed Thursday afternoon that traders see a 45% chance that the Federal Reserve doesn’t cut rates at all in 2026, according to CME Group.  Investors are worried that higher energy prices spurred by the war will push up overall inflation, making it difficult for the Fed to justify rate cuts.

headline: As Iran war heightens affordability issues, don't expect the Fed to 'ride in and save the day,' analyst says

headline: How The Investment Story For CME Group (CME) Is Quietly Shifting On Mixed Analyst Signals
summary: CME Group’s analyst fair value estimate has moved from US$299.29 to US$305.21, a modest reset that has put the stock’s price targets under a fresh spotlight. Recent research pairs this change with a mix of optimism and caution, as analysts weigh stronger modeled commodities volume and product specific upside against concerns that current levels may already reflect a more balanced risk or reward setup. Read on to see how you can track these shifting assumptions and consider what this evolving...
